settings.title: Whole-home renovation of a 1924 craftsman
settings.summary: New systems, a reworked floor plan, and a restored fireplace — a hundred-year-old house set up for its next fifty.
settings.description:
The house had been remuddled twice — dropped ceilings, carpet over fir floors, and a warren of small rooms at the back. The owners wanted it opened up without losing what made it a craftsman.

We took it back to the studs where it mattered: all-new electrical, plumbing, and heating, insulation the house never had, and a rear floor plan reworked around one long sightline from kitchen to garden. The original fir floors came back from under the carpet, and the brick fireplace was repointed rather than replaced.

Five months, one family relocated to a rental we helped them schedule around, and a house that should now outlive everyone involved in renovating it.
